sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: 5am.earth Foundation requests treasury funding to build an open, Cardano-anchored trust layer for global agricultural supply chains combining Veridian self-sovereign identity, a Cardano on-chain satellite oracle, and three application paths across AE certification, traceability/compliance, and finance/credit.
Recipient: 5am.earth Foundation, with pre-Foundation co-promoters Elk GMBH and HashPoint Consulting until registration and Intersect administering treasury contracts on standard terms.

Deliverables: 18-month programme across India, Cambodia, and Kenya, M1 Stand-Up at Month 6 with Foundation operational, 200,000 cumulative farmers registered on Mainnet, AE credentialing live, trust-layer architecture locked, auditor procurement concluded, and legal contract transfer prepared, M2 Closed Loop at Month 12 with 350,000 farmers, three application paths operating against verified trust-layer records, first stablecoin-rail loans disbursed and tracked, and first independent audit, M3 Self-Sustaining at Month 18 with 500,000 cumulative verified farmers, three application paths operating, finance activity on Cardano, AE network credentialed, active Foundation revenue model, and final independent audit, Public on-chain KPI dashboard, quarterly narrative reports, and monthly Cardano Forum updates
Deadline/expiry: 18 months from programme start, with gates at Month 6, Month 12, and Month 18
Claim (adoption, independently_verifiable, high materiality): Project Swaminathan is operating on Cardano Mainnet with 10,500 cumulative farmer and farm registrations as of 5 May 2026.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal says the funded programme will reach 500,000 registered farmers across India, Cambodia, and Kenya by Month 18.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The trust layer will combine farmer and AE identity, verified land and crop data, satellite observations, credentials, application events, and Cardano anchoring into reusable infrastructure for agricultural applications.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The Cardano components are described as already implemented and operational on Project Swaminathan, making the work integration and scaling rather than greenfield development.

Claim (adoption, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The three funded-period application paths are Andamio AE certification, Zengate Global traceability and compliance, and Seedstars SIGMA finance and credit.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The 10,000,000 ADA request is structured as 5,000,000 ADA on approval, 2,000,000 ADA on M1 acceptance, and 3,000,000 ADA on M2 acceptance.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal says the funded programme will reach 500,000 registered farmers across India, Cambodia, and Kenya by Month 18.

Independent evidence for: The Cardano components are described as already implemented and operational on Project Swaminathan, making the work integration and scaling rather than greenfield development.
Independent evidence for: By 2030, the programme projects 3 million farmers, 112.5 million annual on-chain transactions, $900 million TVL, and 16–20 million ADA in annual protocol revenue.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Project Swaminathan is operating on Cardano Mainnet with 10,500 cumulative farmer and farm registrations as of 5 May 2026." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal says the funded programme will reach 500,000 registered farmers across India, Cambodia, and Kenya by Month 18." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
